calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-09-2020 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-02-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Xiuli Chen, T:415.802.9398, plaintiff appearing on her own behalf, with official Court Mandarin Interpreter Connie Chen;
No other appearance.

Ms. Connie Chen disclosed that she is not related to the plaintiff.

RULING: The Court passed the matter and instructed Ms. Connie Chen to translate the tentative ruling for the benefit of the plaintiff who has not read it. The case was recalled and the matter was discussed. After hearing, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT as follows:

Plaintiff, who is representing herself in propria persona, has filed three different actions. Although the complaints are difficult to decipher, each apparently seeks to assert claims arising from plaintiff's medical treatment in 2016 with respect to artificial insemination through in vitro fertilization. Defendant The Regents of the University of California (sued as UCSF Medical Center) filed a demurrer to the complaints and a motion to strike the prayer for punitive damages in each of those actions. Plaintiff did not file or serve a timely opposition to any of the demurrers or motions. Instead, Plaintiff untimely lodged with the court (but did not file) identical 3-page responses, attaching 65 pages of evidentiary materials, with the heading "The defendant's motion and counterclaim are inconsistent with facts and therefore should be invalid." The responses do not present any intelligible argument or authority in opposition to the demurrers or motions.

The demurrer to the first amended complaint in the first-filed of the actions (No. CGC-19-579265 (filed Sept. 16, 2019) is sustained with 30 days leave to amend. Because the filing and pendency of the second and third actions (No. CGC-19-579460 (filed Sept. 23, 2019) and No. CGC-19-579522 (filed Sept. 25, 2019) raising overlapping if not identical claims creates unnecessary burden and expense for the parties and the court, the demurrers to the complaints in those actions are sustained without leave to amend, and those actions are dismissed with prejudice. Any claims that Plaintiff may have against The Regents in connection with her medical treatment must be pled in the first-filed action.

The Court will endeavor to have a Mandarin interpreter available at the hearing, although it is not required to do so and cannot guarantee the availability of an interpreter. Plaintiff is admonished that she is not entitled to special consideration from this court because she is self-represented. "While a party may choose to act as his or her own attorney, such a party is to be treated like any other party and is entitled to the same, but no greater consideration than other litigants and attorneys. Thus, as is the case with attorneys, self-represented litigants must follow correct rules of procedure." (ViaView, Inc. v. Retzlaff (2016) 1 Cal.App.5th 198, 208 (citations and internal quotations omitted).) If Plaintiff fails to file an amended complaint within 30 days of the date of the court's order, the action will be dismissed on appropriate application by The Regents under Code of Civil Procedure section 581(f).

Counsel for defendant is to prepare and submit a proposed order that repeats verbatim the Court's ruling.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
